title: Command Lifecycle, Configuration, Pipeline, and Atomic Output · Behavior Case: Main Boundary
summary: The script reads arguments during import, mixes parsing with writes, partially replaces the public directory on failure, and cannot explain which configuration won. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the main boundary increment in import-safe deterministic build pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice…
objectives: Keep import-safe definitions separate from a main entry point that returns meaningful status and writes diagnostics to the correct stream.
Verify media type, character encoding, caching, compression, conditional delivery, range needs, and error behavior on the target host.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-cli-pipeline-5
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-cli-pipeline-5.
# Case 9036: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def worked_static_site_cli_pipeline_5(stages: int = 10, completed: int = 10, import_safe: bool = True, staged_output: bool = True, prior_artifact_kept: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "readerworked5",
        "source9036",
        "parse5dekrow",
        "routeworked59036",
        "artifact9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if stages < 8 or completed < 0:
        return False, completed, "pipeline-shape"
    if completed != stages:
        return False, completed, "partial-pipeline"
    if not import_safe or not staged_output:
        return False, completed, "effect-boundary"
    if not prior_artifact_kept:
        return False, completed, "destructive-output"
    assert completed == stages
    return True, completed, "atomic-pipeline"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Main Boundary model · Theory Main Boundary
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Main Boundary: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 7352 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to own an import-safe staged build pipeline that preserves the prior artifact on failure; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep import-safe definitions separate from a main entry point that returns meaningful status and writes diagnostics to the correct stream.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Main Boundary model · Theory Main Boundary.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Main Boundary model · Theory Main Boundary, keep Main Boundary connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Keep import-safe definitions separate from a main entry point that returns meaningful status and writes diagnostics to the correct stream.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Main Boundary model · Theory Main Boundary, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Main Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Main Boundary model · Theory Main Boundary, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 7352 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to own an import-safe staged build pipeline that preserves the prior artifact on failure; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep import-safe definitions separate from a main entry point that returns meaningful status and writes diagnostics to the correct stream.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Main Boundary model · Theory Main Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: A command-line script cannot be both importable and easy to run.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-main-boundary-dvps1g(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_main_boundary_dvps1g\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?stages < 8 or completed < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?completed != stages)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not import_safe or not staged_output)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not prior_artifact_kept)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
